subject: english
topic_title: Comprehension Building, Unstressed Vowel /ә/, and Argumentative Essay
source_title: FIRST TERM E-LEARNING NOTE SUBJECT: ENGLISH LANGUAGE CLASS: SS 2
learning_objectives: Summarize the key ideas in Building.
Identify and pronounce Unstressed Vowel /ә/ correctly.
Write a clear Argumentative Essay using the expected features and structure.

sections.heading: Comprehension – Building
sections.content: The passage gives an insight into the processes that are undertaken before a building can be set up. The persons who play vital roles in these processes include the surveyor, foreman and the architect.

sections.heading: Speech Work: Unstressed Vowel /ә/
sections.content: The ‘schwa” as this vowel is called, is a short vowel produced with the lips in neutral position. The vowel is represented by many letters usually in unaccented (unstressed) syllables. Examples: allow (initial), perhaps (medial), sailor (final), understand, teacher, doctor.

sections.heading: Writing – Argumentative Essay
sections.content: An argumentative essay is one that requires you to present a subject with a view to persuading your reader to agree with your own point of view against another one. Features: Heading: preferably written in capitals. Introduction: A paragraph which contains a statement of purpose. Body/Content: At least three well developed paragraphs, each with a topic sentence. Conclusion: (A paragraph) Recommendations.
